stories.headline: Dining on eatery data
stories.summary: The St. Louis Post-Dispatch uses computer-assisted reporting to analyze restaurant health inspection data, revealing that despite high average scores, critical violations are not uncommon and can pose health risks.

stories.headline: EEOC data is goldmine for job stories
stories.summary: A Wall Street Journal investigation of EEOC employment data from over 40 million workers revealed that Black workers were the only ethnic group to suffer net job losses during the 1990-91 recession, losing 59,479 jobs while other groups gained employment. The analysis, which required extensive FOIA requests and verification calls to 400 major federal contractors, has prompted civil rights leaders and a congressional investigation into the companies involved.
